name: Direct Connect Resilience Review
description: Multi-section AWS Direct Connect resilience assessment covering per-DXGW scoring, critical findings, ghost-node recommendations, best-practice checklist, and cost-to-reach-target estimates.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.prompt:
You are writing Section 1 (Executive Summary) of the Direct Connect Resilience Review.

Call `discover_dx_topology` if the topology isn't already in context, then call `assess_dx_resiliency` with default targets. From the returned CombinedAssessment, produce a 4-part summary:

1. **Overall posture** â€” describe the distribution of DXGW tiers (e.g. "2 of 4 gateways at Maximum, 1 at High, 1 at None"). Do NOT invent or quote any aggregate /100 score â€” resiliency is reported per DX Gateway only.
2. **DXGW inventory** â€” total number of DX Gateways, how many are at each tier (none / devtest / high / maximum), and the lowest-tier gateway.
3. **Top findings** â€” 3 highest-severity recommendations from the assessment (critical first, then warning), one short sentence each.
4. **Headline metric** â€” one line summarising whether each gateway currently qualifies for the 99.9% (High) and 99.99% (Maximum) DX SLAs.

Display results as tight markdown. Use a small table for the DXGW tier breakdown. Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.

sections.title: Per-DXGW Assessment
sections.prompt:
You are writing Section 2 (Per-DXGW Assessment) of the Direct Connect Resilience Review.

For every DX Gateway in the `perDxGateway` array returned by `assess_dx_resiliency`, render a short block containing:

- Gateway name and ID (as a heading).
- Current tier + target tier (none / devtest / high / maximum). Do NOT show or invent a /100 score.
- Location count, connection count, AWS logical-device count.
- A short bulleted list of findings scoped to this gateway (severity + title only â€” full descriptions go in later sections).

If the topology has more than 6 DX Gateways, group them by current tier and collapse any gateway already at Maximum into a one-line entry ("Gateway X â€” at Max tier, no action required").

Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.

sections.title: Critical Findings
sections.prompt:
You are writing Section 3 (Critical Findings) of the Direct Connect Resilience Review.

From the aggregated `resiliency.recommendations` and `bestPractice.recommendations` returned by `assess_dx_resiliency`, list every recommendation with severity `critical` or `warning`. For each:

- Severity badge (đź”´ critical, đźźˇ warning).
- Title.
- One-paragraph description quoting the rule's own language.
- Affected resources (DXGW ID, location, VIF ID, etc. â€” extracted from the rule ID or description).

If `critical_findings` resolves to an empty list (everything is `info` severity), output a single line: "No critical or warning-severity findings â€” see Section 5 for advisory items."

Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.

sections.title: Recommended Infrastructure Changes
sections.prompt:
You are writing Section 4 (Recommended Infrastructure Changes) of the Direct Connect Resilience Review.

From the per-DXGW recommendations returned by `assess_dx_resiliency`, describe each ghost-node recommendation in prose:

- What to add (new DX location, second connection, LAG, etc.).
- Where it goes (location code, AWS logical device, DXGW attachment).
- What it unlocks (next SLA tier: 99.9% High â†’ 99.99% Maximum).
- Explicit pre-conditions (needs 2+ AWS logical devices per location for Max tier).

Group by target tier (items needed to reach High first, then Max). If a recommendation has no `additionalNodes` array (best-practice rule with no topology change), skip it here â€” it belongs in Section 5.

Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.

sections.title: Best Practices Checklist
sections.prompt:
You are writing Section 5 (Best Practices Checklist) of the Direct Connect Resilience Review.

From `assess_dx_resiliency`'s best-practice annotations and recommendations, build a table with columns:

| Practice | Status | Affected DXGW | Notes |

Status values: âś… Applied, âš ď¸Ź Gap, âšŞ Verify (attestation-only, cannot be confirmed via AWS API).

Include at least these categories â€” flag any not surfaced by the assessment as âšŞ Verify:

- Two DX locations minimum
- Two connections per location on separate AWS logical devices
- BFD enabled on customer router
- Site-to-Site VPN backup
- Match DX region to resource region
- Continuous monitoring of VIF BGP + connection state
- Regular failover testing
- Use of Direct Connect Gateway
- BGP advertisement limits (100 private / 1000 public)
- Fail-over runbook documented

Group rows by status (Gaps first, then Verify, then Applied). Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.

sections.title: Cost to Reach Target Tier
sections.prompt:
You are writing Section 6 (Cost to Reach Target Tier) of the Direct Connect Resilience Review.

For every DX Gateway that is NOT already at Maximum tier, call `estimate_upgrade_cost` with the gateway's current target (use `assess_dx_resiliency.perDxGateway[].targetLevel`). Render a table:

| DX Gateway | Current Tier | Target Tier | Est. Monthly Delta (USD) | Assumptions |

Under the table, add a total monthly delta summing across gateways, and a short disclaimer: "Rough budgeting estimate only. Excludes data transfer, cross-connect fees, and partner pricing nuances. Confirm with `get_dx_pricing` for exact rates."

If every gateway is at Maximum, output: "All DX Gateways already meet the Maximum Resiliency tier â€” no upgrade cost."

Do NOT include follow-up questions or suggestions.
